ships.name: Akster
ships.description: The turn of the millenium was one of the lowest points in the illustrious history of Lionheart Industries; the company was facing shrinking market share and an unsteady place in the zeitgeist as Tarazed's newer ships captivated formerly-loyal customers. While this slippage would eventually be addressed by the release of the Corvette and Lindwurm, Lionheart produced the Akster medium warship as a limited-edition offering for the millenium celebrations, hoping to recapture eyes and minds and to entice wealthy investors. 	Akstere remain some of the most powerful ships of their class, but were never a profitable product line and so never entered full production. Most these days belong either to the wealthy investors to which they were originally marketed, or to pirates who have 'liberated' the ships for their own use.

ships.name: Akster of the Torches
ships.description: Once the property of the feared leader of the Torches Gang, then the property of Syndicate executive Leo Merced, and now the property of you. For fifteen years the ship was the terror of the galactic north before Merced brough its owner in. It's just about as aggressive as a warship comes; its internal systems are undoubtedly in violation of half a dozen regulatory laws and its weapons layout belongs to a ship twice its size.

ships.name: Erinys
ships.description: The Erinys is a powerful mid-size warship designed by Southbound Shipyards for the Free Worlds, a durable destroyer with oversized engines strong enough to go toe-to-toe with a Republic Navy Frigate and fast enough to harass and waylay a Cruiser. Built of the same high-quality materials as the Dreadnought and equipped with the best the Free Worlds have to offer, a battlegroup of Erinyes is just as fearsome as their mythological epithets would imply.

ships.name: Hydrargyrum
ships.description: Though the Quicksilver's enduring popularity with corporate customers has made it a ubiquitous feature of core space, its spartan design and limited outfitting options have made it a less desirable choice among independent pilots. Megaparsec's new Hydrargyrum light warship is designed along the same lines as the Quicksilver, but can carry up to double its armament, making it among the most powerful warships of its class. Despite this, its interior space remains restrictive, and pilots will have to choose between having effective shield generation and reverse thrust.

ships.name: Lindwurm
ships.description: Produced in tandem with Lionheart's new Corvette medium warship, the sleek and fiery Lindwurm doubles down on the characteristics that made its larger sibling such a success. Though classed as a light warship, the Lindwurm punches well above its size and exhibits unparalleled agility thanks to its lightweight hull; though this does mean that it relies almost entirely on its shields for defense. The premium price tag of these ships puts them squarely out of reach of less successful merchants, but their rapid interception capabilities have made them an effective component of larger freight convoys as well as the internal security services of the Deep. 	The shared names of the Lindwurm and a certain Quarg freighter have not escaped notice, especially in the more southerly areas of human space. Though Lionheart corporate dismisses this as a mere cultural coincidence, one of the Lindwurm's lead designers claims - to some ridicule - that the Quarg, in fact, stole the name from her.

ships.name: Hekla Dora
ships.description: The Hekla is a traditional design from the shipyards of Thule, only a very distant cousin to any other ship in human space. Even though Thule is now well-enough connected to the rest of the galaxy, these still lag a generation or so behind the craft coming out of larger-name shipyards, in exchange for being reliable and exceptionally versatile. Most of the ships are etched by the lead shipwright in charge of manufacturing them, and despite the basic design being ubiquitous on Thule, many operators swear by their preferred builder as giving them an extra edge. The Dora version is popular among freelance miners for its pair of drone bays and large cargo hold, though it can also function as a combat carrier if equipped with armed drones.

ships.name: Hekla Vaba
ships.description: The Hekla is a traditional design from the shipyards of Thule, only a very distant cousin to any other ship in human space. Even though Thule is now well-enough connected to the rest of the galaxy, these still lag a generation or so behind the craft coming out of larger-name shipyards, in exchange for being reliable and exceptionally versatile. Most of the ships are etched by the lead shipwright in charge of manufacturing them, and despite the basic design being ubiquitous on Thule, many operators swear by their preferred builder as giving them an extra edge. The Vaba version emphasises direct combat ability, gaining an additional turret mount and stronger shields. While one of these craft won't quite stand up to the likes of a Bastion or Frigate, they continue to be popular among southwestern pirates for their cargo space and hardpoint flexibility.

ships.name: Moonbeam
ships.description: The design has long been out of production and the ship's notoriously temperamental power systems have kept it from enjoying the same enduring popularity as designs like the Firebird and Berserker. Nonetheless, the ample engine bays and lightweight hull of the Moonbeam have made it a rare and beloved possession of the few lucky enough to still have one; these old ships are still the fastest heavy warships in the sky.

ships.name: Moonbeam of the Serrated Blade
ships.description: The design has long been out of production and the ship's notoriously temperamental power systems have kept it from enjoying the same enduring popularity as designs like the Firebird and Berserker. Nonetheless, the ample engine bays and lightweight hull of the Moonbeam have made it a rare and beloved possession of the few lucky enough to still have one; these old ships are still the fastest heavy warships in the sky. 	Our Lady of the Serrated Blade has had this particular Moonbeam modified, upon request, to boost power to its aging shield matrix. Note that this has increased the stress on the ship's already-sensitive power bus, making it especially vulnerable to ion weaponry.

ships.name: Reflection
ships.description: First built by industrial manufacturer Janus Systems in the early 2930s, the Reflection class of intra-system tugs was designed to reposition space stations, fielding an astonishingly massive engine block. Though Janus went bankrupt by the 2980s, the occasional well-maintained Reflection still travels the stars, though by now they have mostly landed in the hands of pirates and other unscrupulous figures who have converted them into either rapid freighters or unusual, if effective, medium warships. 	Large forward-mounted radiators, designed to radiate engine heat at low velocity, give the Reflection excellent thermal performance at the cost of greatly weakening its hull.

ships.name: Marauder Reflection
ships.description: With a haphazard helping of additional armor plating, refurbished aetherdynamic engine nozzles, a strengthened shield grid, and double the number of gun ports, this pirate's special Reflection is capable of standing up to even modern warships.

ships.name: Catatumbo
ships.description: Bristling with weapons hardpoints and humming with electrifying potential, the Catatumbo completes the Wanderers' heavy warship trifecta. Where the Derecho emphasizes speed and the Hurricane durability, this warship has instead experienced an aggressive metamorphosis from its original, ancient form, now reintroduced to the modern era like a virulent and invasive predator.

ships.name: Rime Branch
ships.description: The crackle of frost on a winter's morning, the breath of wind through layers of chilly air; these are moments worth remembering in the heat of a fight. 	The Rime Branch is a small Wanderer patrol vessel, a slightly larger alternative to the Autumn Leaf that enjoys additional durability and outfit capacity without compromising its impressive mobility.

variants.name: Hydrargyrum (Expensive)
variants.description: Though the Quicksilver's enduring popularity with corporate customers has made it a ubiquitous feature of core space, its spartan design and limited outfitting options have made it a less desirable choice among independent pilots. Megaparsec's new Hydrargyrum light warship is designed along the same lines as the Quicksilver, but can carry up to double its armament, making it among the most powerful warships of its class. Despite this, its interior space remains restrictive, and pilots will have to choose between having effective shield generation and reverse thrust.

Large forward-mounted radiators, designed to radiate engine heat at low velocity, give the Reflection excellent thermal performance at the cost of greatly weakening its hull.

variants.name: Mining Drone (Violent)
variants.description: The semi-autonomous Mining Drone is used by the Syndicate alongside the Sunder mining vessel in a supportive role. Not being much more than a Mining Laser with an engine stuck to the rear of it, these drones do not last long in combat with other ships due to how fragile they are. 	Drones do not come equipped with a hyperdrive. You cannot carry a drone unless you have a ship in your fleet with a drone bay.

variants.name: Catatumbo (Beam)
variants.description: Bristling with weapons hardpoints and humming with electrifying potential, the Catatumbo completes the Wanderers' heavy warship trifecta.
